title: Tamil Idli

ingredients.name: fermented idli-dosa batter
visual_checks: store-bought or home-fermented overnight; see cultural note
ingredients.notes: store-bought or home-fermented overnight; see cultural note

subs.name: Soak 2 cups idli rice and 0.5 cup urad dal separately overnight; drain, grind urad dal first until fluffy, then rice into a coarse paste, combine and ferment 8–10 hours in a warm spot.
subs.notes: from-scratch batter is noticeably more flavourful

ingredients.name: oil
visual_checks: to grease idli molds; ghee makes them richer
ingredients.notes: to grease idli molds; ghee makes them richer

subs.name: ghee
subs.notes: adds a subtle richness; traditional Brahmin idlis use ghee in the molds

steps.text: Pour 400 ml water into the base of an idli steamer or a large pot fitted with a steamer rack. Set over high flame and bring to a vigorous boil.
If the batter has been refrigerated, rest it at room temperature for 20 minutes until it loosens. Stir in salt and mix gently — the batter should pour like thick cream, coating the back of a spoon.
Lightly grease each idli cup with a dab of oil using your fingertip or a pastry brush. Even coverage prevents the idlis from tearing when you scoop them out.
Ladle batter into each mold, filling to three-quarters — they will puff during steaming and a full mold will overflow. Lower the rack into the steamer.
Cover tightly and steam on medium-high flame for 10–12 minutes. Pierce the centre of one idli with a toothpick; it should emerge clean and dry. If batter clings, steam for 2 more minutes.
Remove the rack and rest for 2 minutes before scooping the idlis out with a wet spoon or knife. They will release easily when the steam has settled. Serve immediately with sambar and coconut chutney.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: idli-dosa-batter → Soak 2 cups idli rice and 0.5 cup urad dal separately overnight; drain, grind urad dal first until fluffy, then rice into a coarse paste, combine and ferment 8–10 hours in a warm spot. (from-scratch batter is noticeably more flavourful); oil → ghee (adds a subtle richness; traditional Brahmin idlis use ghee in the molds)
